title: Yield Data Portability: Which Farm Platforms Let Your Data Out (Matrix v0)
description: Format-by-format: how yield data leaves Deere, FieldView, Case IH, Precision Planting and Trimble — ISOXML, shapefile, and the proprietary containers between.
body_html:
Yield data portability is your ability to take the harvest data your own combine generated and use it outside the platform that collected it — in another FMIS, a spreadsheet, an agronomist's GIS, or a free tool like our Yield Map Viewer. The blunt state of it in 2026: every major vendor's yield log leaves the machine in a proprietary container first (Deere's JD-Data folder, FieldView's .dat, CNH's .cn1, Precision Planting's .2020, Trimble's AgData), and the only format everything accepts on the way in is the humble shapefile.

Compatibility matrix v0 (export / import, cited or marked UNVERIFIED)

PlatformNative machine containerOpen-format EXPORTIMPORT accepts
John Deere Gen4 / Ops CenterJD-Data folder (USB)Boundaries as shapefile from Ops Center; ISOXML task data via the TC-GEO activation (marketed for Europe); G5 ISOXML export UNVERIFIED; native yield-layer export from Ops Center UNVERIFIEDPrescriptions as shapefile (Rx folder at USB root)
Climate FieldView.dat (proprietary)Partner API: GeoJSON (documented for planting summaries); farmer-facing yield shapefile export UNVERIFIEDRx + boundaries as zipped shapefile (WGS-84); 20|20 .dat via email-to-app
Case IH AFS Connect.cn1 folderPortal reports as PDF/CSV/XLS; direct shapefile/ISOXML yield export UNVERIFIEDShapefile (AFS Pro 1200 manual, p.537)
Precision Planting 20|20 (PTx).2020 file (USB)Via FieldView module or Panorama sharingRx as .2020 or shapefile set
PTx Trimble / FarmENGAGEAgData/AgGPSTask data as ISOXML, AgData, John Deere, CNH .cn1, AgLeader — the broadest converter in this matrixTask data from ISOXML, JD, .cn1, Raven, AgLeader, 20|20

The standards that matter

  • ISOXML (ISO 11783-10) — the TASKDATA.XML + binary timelog standard that task controllers and FMIS exchange. It's what our free browser-based import tool reads: drop a TASKDATA zip, see your yield points, nothing uploads.
  • ADAPT (AgGateway) — the conversion framework (500k+ plugin downloads) now evolving into the ADAPT Standard: v1.0.0 June 2024, v2.0.0 June 2025 (high-definition boundaries for autonomy), v2.0.2 August 2025 — the current release as of July 2026.
  • Shapefile — 30-year-old GIS format, still the lingua franca for prescriptions and boundaries on every platform above.

What farmers actually say

Our mined app-store review corpus is blunt: FieldView reviewers warn to "download your data first" before canceling, and 58% of the 74 recent FieldView reviews we analyzed are 1-star — the full numbers are on get your data out of FieldView and exporting yield data across 5 platforms. The repair side just moved (see the July 2026 FTC–Deere settlement) — the data side hasn't.

Matrix cells cite vendor documentation fetched July 18, 2026; UNVERIFIED marks mean we could not confirm from a primary source — corrections welcome via the site repo.
